executionPhases: 1. Proceed to first behaviorally equivalent duplication consolidation
3. Shared interface contracts for same-name/different-semantics capabilities

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Family vocabulary and cue polarity map per age band.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #303 semantic-boundary gate: policy-contaminated semantic split; do not force parity-preserving consolidation.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Adult canonical cue path and Teen signal path do not emit equivalent normalized canonical evidence prior to policy.

rows.architectureStability: Adult and Teen currently derive different canonical evidence records from the same documents before policy (validated by #303 semantic-boundary and semantic-core-only gates).
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen only if recommendation philosophy is unified or a new shared semantic ontology replaces age-specific cue models.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Identity rejection categories and confidence thresholds per age band.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #204 role gate: treat as shared identity-assessment contract with separate age-policy enforcement; do not force parity-preserving consolidation.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Adult combines broad final-slate audit diagnostics with identity enforcement, while Teen applies publication-identity as direct final-eligibility policy with narrower classification semantics.

rows.architectureStability: Role/equivalence gates show similar naming but materially different semantics or decision boundaries.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if policy wrappers are redesigned around a shared semantic contract with deterministic parity evidence.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Tier definitions and underfill policy per age band.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #205 role gate: Adult and Teen counterfactuals answer different hypothetical questions; define contract only and avoid parity-preserving consolidation.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Teen counterfactual models tiered final-slate composition (strong/secondary/weak-underfill and query-derived evidence treatment), while Adult counterfactual models weighted-polarity family decisions per candidate. They answer different hypothetical questions despite similar naming.

rows.architectureStability: Role/equivalence gates show similar naming but materially different semantics or decision boundaries.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if policy wrappers are redesigned around a shared semantic contract with deterministic parity evidence.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Definition of decision-worthy candidate by age band.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #306 role gate: shared query-quality mechanism with age-specific policy output differences; keep separate wrappers.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Query-quality core appears shared, but age-band policy changes resulting per-query quality outputs.

rows.architectureStability: Query-quality telemetry collection and core metrics are shared, while age-band policy interpretation diverges downstream.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if age-band quality interpretation is unified or query-quality policy moves into a single cross-band decision model.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Family competition objective function and early-stop policy.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #307 role gate: family competition uses shared mechanism with age-specific interpretation.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Family competition depends on shared per-query telemetry, while age-band policy changes comparative family outcomes.

rows.architectureStability: Query-quality telemetry collection and core metrics are shared, while age-band policy interpretation diverges downstream.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if age-band quality interpretation is unified or query-quality policy moves into a single cross-band decision model.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Decision-worthy class mapping per age band.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #308 role gate: Adult and Teen marginal-yield analyses answer different questions; keep separate.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Adult and Teen metrics optimize different objectives despite similar query-performance vocabulary.

rows.architectureStability: Role/equivalence gates show similar naming but materially different semantics or decision boundaries.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if policy wrappers are redesigned around a shared semantic contract with deterministic parity evidence.

rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #409 role gate: query promotion/replacement runs on a shared mechanism with age-specific policy interpretation.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Adult and Teen share the same query promotion/replacement execution order and attempted-query mechanism.

rows.architectureStability: Query-quality telemetry collection and core metrics are shared, while age-band policy interpretation diverges downstream.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if age-band quality interpretation is unified or query-quality policy moves into a single cross-band decision model.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Component weights and minimum confidence per age band.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #410 role gate: narrative-strength responsibilities are in different pipeline layers.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Narrative-strength scoring is production ranking in Adult but upstream confidence support in Teen, i.e., different pipeline layer responsibilities.

rows.architectureStability: Role/equivalence gates show similar naming but materially different semantics or decision boundaries.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if policy wrappers are redesigned around a shared semantic contract with deterministic parity evidence.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Tier thresholds and provenance guardrails per age band.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Shared interface contract adopted with unchanged age-band behavior and explicit semantic differences documented.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #411 role gate: meaningful taste tiers answer different age-band questions.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Adult and Teen meaningful-taste models answer different selection questions despite overlapping terminology.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Explicitly separate adult mature-content policy from teen safety policy.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Capability remains separate; policy invariants and tests prevent accidental unification.
rows.recommendation: Reclassified by #599 role gate: keep adult content weighting as explicit age-specific policy branch.

reclassificationEvidence.rationale: Age-band guard + adult-only weighted polarity logic indicate intentional age-specific policy branch.

rows.architectureStability: Capability is intentionally age-specific and tied to policy constraints.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen only if age-band policy separation is intentionally removed.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Teen provenance policy: forbid query/route/profile context; allow only candidate-native + verified metadata.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Not applicable. Closed by Phase 0 novelty proof (noSubstantiveDifference).
rows.recommendation: Closed/falsified. Skip implementation and proceed to first parity-preserving consolidation.

preflightCheckpoint.title: #101 novelty proof
preflightCheckpoint.rationale: Prior Teen trusted-reconciliation counterfactual showed 0 weak-to-secondary promotions and no weak-underfill/slate improvements.
noveltyProofRequirements: List Adult production inputs/transformations not present in prior Teen trusted-reconciliation counterfactual.
Identify newly available evidence fields and newly accepted provenance types (if any).
Identify changed pipeline stage and whether behavior impacts acquisition/query continuation vs post-hoc reclassification.
Provide candidate-level examples where evidence would differ and why prior 0-promotion result does not already falsify #101.

decisionRule.noSubstantiveDifference: Mark #101 as already falsified by prior counterfactual; skip production integration and continue with first parity-preserving consolidation.
decisionRule.substantiveDifference: Run diagnostic corpus counterfactual for the new behavior and evaluate existing #101 acceptance criteria.
decisionRule.architecturalOnlyDifference: Reclassify #101 as maintenance work, not recommendation-quality experiment.

rows.architectureStability: Prior counterfactual and novelty-proof outcomes show no substantive untested behavior difference.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen only if upstream architecture introduces new evidence channels or stage placement that invalidates novelty assumptions.

rows.agePolicyConfigurationRequired: Age-band lexicon + generic-context blacklist passed by config.
rows.acceptanceCriteria: Adult and Teen outputs are bit-for-bit identical pre/post refactor (decisions, ranking order, diagnostics, reason codes).
rows.recommendation: Extract shared primitive and parametrize age policy
rows.architectureStability: Parity signature is locked and comparator enforces zero-drift behavior.
rows.architectureFutureTrigger: Reopen if lexicon/token-boundary architecture changes or parity signature drift is intentionally introduced.
